entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: An important oilseed crop grown in India, also known as Arachis hypogaea L.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the groundnut crop is grown and the roving surveys were conducted.

entities.name: STEM ROT
entities.description: A disease of groundnut caused by the pathogen S. rolfsii.

entities.name: SCILEROTIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing stem rot in groundnut.

entities.name: FUNGAL BIOCONTROL AGENTS
entities.description: Potent fungal agents identified for the effective management of stem rot.

entities.name: BACTERIAL BIOCONTROL AGENTS
entities.description: Potent bacterial agents identified for the effective management of stem rot.

entities.name: ROVING SURVEY
entities.description: A survey conducted in major groundnut growing areas to record the incidence of stem rot.

entities.name: KHARIF
entities.description: The agricultural season during which the roving surveys were conducted in 2013 and 2014.

entities.name: GUJARAT
entities.description: A state in India that recorded the highest incidence of stem rot.

entities.name: TELANGANA
entities.description: A state in India that documented the least incidence of stem rot.

entities.name: MAHARASHTRA
entities.description: A state in India that recorded comparatively higher incidence of stem rot.

entities.name: TAMIL NADU
entities.description: A state in India that recorded comparatively higher incidence of stem rot.

entities.name: BLACK SOILS
entities.description: Soil type associated with higher incidence of stem rot when susceptible cultivars are grown as a sole crop.

entities.name: RICHARDS'S BROTH
entities.description: A medium found to be the best for supporting in vitro oxalic acid production by S. rolfsii.

entities.name: OXALIC ACID PRODUCTION
entities.description: A biochemical trait of S. rolfsii isolates that is positively correlated with virulence.

entities.name: IN VITRO
entities.description: The experimental condition used for measuring oxalic acid production.

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE STUDIES
entities.description: The experimental setup used to determine the most effective inoculum level of S. rolfsii.

entities.name: PATHOGENICITY
entities.description: The ability of S. rolfsii isolates to cause disease, with all 60 isolates found to be pathogenic.

entities.name: VIRULENCE
entities.description: The degree of pathogenicity of S. rolfsii isolates, categorized into highly virulent and less virulent.

entities.name: GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The speed of mycelial growth of S. rolfsii isolates, ranging from 0.66 to 1.29 mm/hr.

entities.name: BIOMASS PRODUCTION
entities.description: The amount of biomass produced by S. rolfsii isolates, ranging from 6.82 to 14.62 mg/day.

entities.name: SCLEROTIA
entities.description: Hardened fungal masses produced by all S. rolfsii isolates on PDA medium.

entities.name: PDA MEDIUM
entities.description: The medium used to observe the production of sclerotia by S. rolfsii isolates.

entities.name: MYCELIAL GROWTH TYPE
entities.description: The morphological growth pattern of S. rolfsii isolates, categorized as highly profuse or profuse.
